phase1: Today is [CURRENT_DATE]. ⏰ SESSION & DATA RELEASE STATUS CHECK (MANDATORY FIRST STEP — complete this before any other analysis): (1) List ALL major US, European, and Asian macro data releases and central-bank events scheduled for today [CURRENT_DATE] (include exact release times in UTC). (2) For each release, determine whether it has ALREADY OCCURRED (post-data) or is still PENDING (pre-data). (3) Output a clear status line: If pending: 'PRE-DATA BIAS — [Event Name] pending at [HH:MM UTC] — bias carries LOWER CONFIDENCE until release. Reduce final Phase 6 confidence by at least 1 point per pending Tier-1 event (NFP, CPI, FOMC).' If all released: 'POST-DATA BIAS — All major releases confirmed. Standard confidence scoring applies.' This status line MUST be referenced and reflected in the Phase 6 Confidence score. Output: - Scheduled Events Today: [list each event + release time UTC + status: OCCURRED / PENDING] - Session Status: [PRE-DATA / POST-DATA / MIXED] | Confidence Adjustment: [state penalty if any]  Browse the live web for the latest Fed and Bank of Canada (BoC) policy data. Fetch and report: - Fed current stance + next meeting rate-cut probability (from CME FedWatch — exact %) - BoC current stance + next meeting rate-cut probability (from OIS swap pricing or market consensus — exact %) - Fed Terminal Rate Expectation: where is the Fed Funds rate expected to settle by year-end? (from CME FedWatch year-end strip or consensus forecasts — exact %) - BoC Terminal Rate Expectation: where is the BoC Overnight Rate expected to settle by year-end? (from OIS pricing or analyst consensus — exact %) - Any Fed or BoC speaker comments in the last 48 hours — specifically note any remarks from Powell (Fed) or Macklem (BoC) or other voting members - Which central bank is more hawkish right now and why ⚠ ENHANCED ANTI-HALLUCINATION PROTOCOL (APPLIES TO EVERY DATA POINT IN THIS PHASE): (A) SOURCE CITATION RULE — For EVERY exact figure you report (percentages, price levels, contract counts, barrel figures, yield levels, PMI values, etc.), you MUST append the source domain immediately after the figure in parentheses — e.g., '3.2% (Source: bls.gov)', '$82.5/bbl (Source: Reuters)', '223K (Source: bls.gov)'. Acceptable authoritative sources: bls.gov, eia.gov, federalreserve.gov, ecb.europa.eu, cmegroup.com, investing.com, reuters.com, bloomberg.com, wsj.com, ft.com, lme.com, coinglass.com, glassnode.com, defillama.com, alternative.me, farside.co.uk. (B) DATA UNAVAILABLE RULE — If you cannot confidently cite a named reliable source for a specific figure, output 'Data Unavailable' — do NOT estimate, round, or extrapolate. A missing figure is far less harmful than a fabricated one. (C) FALLBACK RULE — If live web browsing is blocked, returns an error, or yields no usable results for this phase, do NOT guess or hallucinate any numbers. Instead: state 'Relying on macro trend — live data blocked' at the top of your output for this phase, provide only directional trend commentary based on your most recently known context, label every such statement with '(Trend estimate — unverified)', and set the Phase 6 Confidence score to a maximum of 5/10. Output (bullet points only, no paragraphs): - Fed Stance: [hawkish/dovish] | Cut Probability Next Meeting: [X%] | Year-End Terminal Rate: [X%] - BoC Stance: [hawkish/dovish] | Cut Probability Next Meeting: [X%] | Year-End Terminal Rate: [X%] - Rate Differential by Year-End: [Fed Terminal – BoC Terminal = bps gap] — widening or narrowing vs current? - Dominant CB: [Fed/BoC/Equal] - USDCAD Policy Bias: [Bullish/Bearish/Neutral] — one line reason Compressed summary block (mandatory — paste this into Phase 2): ``` [P1 - POLICY DIVERGENCE] - Fed Outlook: [stance + next meeting cut probability % + year-end terminal rate %] - BoC Outlook: [stance + next meeting cut probability % + year-end terminal rate %] - Year-End Rate Gap: [Fed terminal – BoC terminal in bps, widening/narrowing] - Key Speaker Note: [most recent Powell/Macklem/Fed/BoC comment in 48h, or None] - Core Logic: [why this divergence + terminal rate gap matters for USDCAD today] - Bias: [Bullish / Bearish / Neutral] ```

Fetch and report (only confirmed released data): - US: CPI, NFP, GDP, PMI — state Actual vs Forecast vs Previous for each (mark "Data Unavailable" if not yet released) - Canada: Canadian CPI (headline + core), Employment Change (net jobs added), GDP (monthly or quarterly), and Ivey PMI — state Actual vs Forecast vs Previous for each (mark "Data Unavailable" if not yet released) - Citigroup Economic Surprise Index for USD and CAD (exact values — positive = beating expectations; state "Data Unavailable" if not found) - Any high-impact data releases scheduled TODAY that are a risk for USDCAD 
⚙ MARKET REGIME IDENTIFICATION (MANDATORY — output this before the compressed summary block): Based on the data you have collected in this phase AND the prevailing macro narrative, identify the current Market Regime from the following categories: (A) 'Inflation Fears' — the market is currently punishing hot economic data; bond yields are rising because of inflation, not growth; rate-cut expectations are falling; 'bad news is bad news' and 'good news is bad news' for risk assets. (B) 'Growth / Recession Fears' — the market is worried about economic slowdown or recession; weak data signals demand destruction, not rate relief; 'bad news is bad news' for both bonds and risk assets. (C) 'Liquidity / Rate-Cut Optimism' — the market is in 'bad news is good news' mode; soft data raises rate-cut probabilities; risk assets rally on weak prints because easier policy is the dominant narrative. (D) 'Mixed / Transitional' — conflicting signals; the regime is unclear or shifting. IMPORTANT: The identified regime MUST be used to correctly interpret every data point in this phase. For example: in regime (C), a weak PMI print should be flagged as potentially BULLISH for risk assets (more cuts), NOT bearish. Output: - Current Market Regime: [Inflation Fears / Growth-Recession Fears / Liquidity-Rate-Cut Optimism / Mixed-Transitional] - Regime Interpretation: [one sentence — how this regime changes the reading of today's data vs face value] Output (bullet points only, no paragraphs): - US Momentum: [Positive/Negative/Mixed] — top 2 drivers (confirmed data only) - CAD Momentum: [Positive/Negative/Mixed] — top 2 drivers (confirmed data only) - Citi USD Surprise: [value or "Data Unavailable"] | Citi CAD Surprise: [value or "Data Unavailable"] - Fundamental Upper Hand: [USD / CAD / Neutral] Compressed summary block (mandatory — paste this into Phase 2): ``` [P3 - ECONOMIC MOMENTUM] - US Momentum: [Positive/Negative/Mixed] | Top Driver: [data release + beat/miss, confirmed only] - CAD Momentum: [Positive/Negative/Mixed] | Top Driver: [data release + beat/miss, confirmed only] - Citi USD Surprise Index: [value or "Data Unavailable"] | Citi CAD Surprise Index: [value or "Data Unavailable"] - Market Regime: [Inflation Fears / Growth-Recession Fears / Liquidity-Rate-Cut Optimism / Mixed-Transitional] | Regime Interpretation: [one line] 
- Core Logic: [which economy is outperforming and what it means for USDCAD] - Bias: [Bullish / Bearish / Neutral] ```

📊 FX OPTIONS EXPIRIES CHECK — USDCAD (MANDATORY FOR FOREX SENTIMENT): Browse for any large FX options expiries or option barrier cuts scheduled for TODAY [CURRENT_DATE] at key strike prices for USDCAD. The two primary cut times are: (1) Tokyo Cut: 02:00 UTC — primarily used for Asian-session options. (2) New York Cut (NY Cut): 15:00 UTC — the dominant global cut time, where the largest notional options expire. SOURCES: Check DTCC swap data repository, Reuters IFR Markets options commentary, CME Group FX options data, or Bloomberg FX options scanner. LOGIC: — Large open interest at a key strike acts as a PRICE MAGNET in the hours leading up to the cut — dealers hedge their gamma exposure by pushing price toward the strike. — Once a large strike expires, the 'gravitational pull' disappears and price can move freely — this is often a source of sharp post-cut volatility. — A large option BARRIER (knock-in or knock-out) at a key level can make price action 'sticky' near that barrier — it acts as a WALL rather than a magnet. If no significant expiries are found, state: 'No significant USDCAD options expiries noted for today.' ⚠ ANTI-HALLUCINATION: Do NOT fabricate strikes or notional sizes. If you cannot find confirmed data, state 'Options expiry data unavailable — check IFR Markets or CME for confirmation.' Output additions: - USDCAD NY Cut Options (15:00 UTC): [strike level + notional size in USD/EUR millions, or 'No significant expiry'] (Source: [domain]) | Effect: [Price Magnet at [level] / Barrier Wall at [level] / No notable effect] - USDCAD Tokyo Cut Options (02:00 UTC): [strike level + notional, or 'No significant expiry'] | Effect: [Magnet / Wall / None] - Options Expiry Directional Bias Implication: [Bullish USDCAD — magnet above / Bearish USDCAD — magnet below / Neutral — no dominant expiry] Add to compressed summary block: - Options Expiries (USDCAD): [NY Cut strike + size / 'None significant'] | [Tokyo Cut strike + size / 'None significant'] | Bias Implication: [Bullish/Bearish/Neutral] Output (bullet points only, no paragraphs): - CFTC Net CAD: [contracts + long/short] | Macro Trend Context: [Bullish/Bearish/Neutral macro CAD positioning] - IG Retail: [X% Long / Y% Short] | Contrarian Intraday Signal: [Yes → [direction] / No] - VIX: [level] | S&P 500: [direction] - CNN Fear & Greed: [level + reading] - Overall Sentiment Bias: [Bullish/Bearish/Contrarian] — one line reason Compressed summary block (mandatory — paste this into Phase 2): ``` [P4 - SENTIMENT & POSITIONING] - CFTC Non-Comm Net CAD: [contracts + long/short + extreme? Yes/No] | Macro Context: [trend direction] - IG Retail Sentiment: [X% Long / Y% Short] | Contrarian Intraday Signal: [Yes → [direction] / No] - VIX: [level] | S&P 500: [direction] | Risk Mode: [Risk-On/Risk-Off] - Core Logic: [contrarian intraday signal from IG + macro trend context from COT — which edge applies today] - Bias: [Bullish / Bearish / Contrarian] ```
phase5: Today is [CURRENT_DATE]. Browse the live web for the latest macro and geopolitical risks affecting USDCAD. ⚠ CRITICAL CONTEXT: Canada is a major oil-exporting nation. The Canadian Dollar (CAD) is a petro-currency — it has a strong direct correlation with crude oil prices. When oil rises, CAD typically strengthens (USDCAD falls). When oil falls, CAD typically weakens (USDCAD rises). This is the PRIMARY commodity driver for CAD and MUST be the central focus of this prompt. Fetch and report: - WTI Crude Oil (primary CAD driver): current price (exact), today's directional move (Up/Down X%), and reason for the move if available. Is WTI rising or falling? Map the impact directly to CAD: rising WTI = CAD bullish pressure (USDCAD bearish), falling WTI = CAD bearish pressure (USDCAD bullish). State the implied direction for USDCAD clearly. - Brent Crude Oil (secondary confirmation): current price (exact) and today's directional move (Up/Down X%). Does Brent confirm or contradict WTI's signal? - Gold (XAUUSD) current price, today's directional move (Up/Down X%), and its sentiment/trend context — is Gold rising (safe-haven demand active / risk-off) or falling (risk appetite returning / risk-on)? Correlate this with the current USDCAD directional bias: does rising Gold (risk-off / stronger USD) support or contradict the bias? - Any US or Canadian political or fiscal headlines in the last 24 hours (including USMCA/trade, Canadian budget, US tariff policy affecting Canada) - Any geopolitical flashpoints causing safe-haven USD flow or commodity supply disruption - Any BoC speeches, Fed speeches, Treasury auctions, or major scheduled events TODAY that could spike USDCAD volatility - Rate the overall risk environment: Low / Medium / High / Extreme ⚠ ENHANCED ANTI-HALLUCINATION PROTOCOL (APPLIES TO EVERY DATA POINT IN THIS PHASE): (A) SOURCE CITATION RULE — For EVERY exact figure you report (percentages, price levels, contract counts, barrel figures, yield levels, PMI values, etc.), you MUST append the source domain immediately after the figure in parentheses — e.g., '3.2% (Source: bls.gov)', '$82.5/bbl (Source: Reuters)', '223K (Source: bls.gov)'. phase6_master_bias:
Today is [CURRENT_DATE]. Synthesize the 5 fundamental research inputs below into a clear USDCAD institutional intraday bias.

Research Inputs:
1. Policy Divergence (Fed vs BoC): [P1_OUTPUT]
2. Yield Differentials (US Treasuries vs Canadian Bonds): [P2_OUTPUT]
3. Economic Momentum (US vs Canada): [P3_OUTPUT]
4. Sentiment & Positioning (CAD COT + USDCAD IG): [P4_OUTPUT]
5. Macro & Geopolitical Risk (WTI Oil · Brent · Gold): [P5_OUTPUT]

⚡ STEP 0 — DOMINANT THEME IDENTIFICATION & DYNAMIC WEIGHTING (MANDATORY — complete this before synthesising the 5 pillars): Identify the single Dominant Market Theme driving price action today. Choose the most accurate label: 'Tier-1 Macro Data Release (NFP/CPI/GDP/FOMC)', 'Central Bank Policy Shift', 'Safe-Haven / Risk-Off Flow Event', 'Geopolitical Escalation / De-escalation', 'Institutional Flow Surge (ETF/COT)', 'On-Chain / Tokenomics Catalyst', 'Liquidity / Rate-Cut Repricing', 'Commodity Supply Shock', or 'No Clear Dominant Theme — Weight Equally'. WEIGHTING RULE: The pillar associated with the Dominant Theme should receive approximately 60% of the decision weight. The remaining 4 pillars share the remaining 40% equally (~10% each). OVERRIDE RULE: If a Tier-1 macro event (NFP, CPI, FOMC decision, or equivalent central-bank rate decision) has occurred today or is PENDING today, you MUST explicitly override and disregard short-term retail sentiment signals (IG Client Sentiment, CNN Fear & Greed) for the directional bias — these contrarian retail signals become statistically unreliable around high-impact macro events. State: 'Retail sentiment overridden — Tier-1 macro event active.' Output: - Dominant Theme: [theme label] - Dominant Pillar: [which of the 5 pillars maps to this theme, e.g., 'Pillar 3'] - Dominant Pillar Weight: [~60%] | Remaining Pillars: [~10% each] - Retail Sentiment Override: [Yes — Tier-1 macro event active / No] 
🔄 STEP 1 — MARKET REGIME ALIGNMENT CHECK (MANDATORY): State the current Market Regime: ['Inflation Fears' / 'Growth/Recession Fears' / 'Liquidity/Rate-Cut Optimism' / 'Mixed']. Then verify that your final directional bias logically aligns with this regime: — In 'Inflation Fears': strong economic data = fewer cuts = bearish risk assets / bearish precious metals / bearish bonds. — In 'Growth/Recession Fears': weak data = demand destruction = bearish commodities and risk assets; strong data = relief rally. — In 'Liquidity/Rate-Cut Optimism': weak data = more cuts = bullish risk assets, crypto, and gold; treat 'bad news as good news'. If your bias contradicts the prevailing regime without a specific overriding catalyst, lower confidence by 1 point and explain the contradiction. Output: - Market Regime: [label] - Regime-Bias Alignment: [Aligned / Contradicts regime — reason] 
Instructions:
- Check for contradictions across the 5 inputs. If 2 or more major factors conflict, lower confidence by at least 2 points and state why.
- If all factors align, confirm alignment and justify higher confidence.
- Pay special attention to WTI Crude Oil direction (P5) as it is the PRIMARY driver for CAD — a strongly bullish or bearish oil move can override other factors.
- Recommend the best time-window to trade this bias today based on scheduled events and session liquidity.

Output (bullet points only, no long paragraphs):
- Directional Bias: [Strong Bullish / Mild Bullish / Neutral / Mild Bearish / Strong Bearish]
- Confidence: [X/10] — adjusted down if contradictions found
- Bias Type: [Trend-Following / Mean-Reversion / Contrarian]
- Primary Driver 1: [top factor + why it dominates today]
- Primary Driver 2: [second factor + why it matters]
- Dominant Theme: [theme label]
- Dominant Pillar Weight: [~60% to dominant pillar | ~10% each to others]
- Retail Sentiment Override: [Yes — Tier-1 macro event active / No]
- Market Regime: [Inflation Fears / Growth-Recession Fears / Liquidity-Rate-Cut Optimism / Mixed]
- Regime-Bias Alignment: [Aligned / Contradicts — reason]
- Contradiction Check: [list any conflicting signals, or "None — all factors aligned"]
- Invalidation Point: [what specific event or data shift would flip this bias]
- Optimal Trading Window: [e.g., session + time UTC + reason]

Compressed summary block (mandatory — paste this into Steps 2.2 and 2.3):
```
[USDCAD MASTER BIAS - [CURRENT_DATE]]
- Directional Bias: [Strong Bullish / Mild Bullish / Neutral / Mild Bearish / Strong Bearish]
- Confidence: [X/10]
- Bias Type: [Trend-Following / Mean-Reversion / Contrarian]
- Primary Driver 1: [factor + logic]
- Primary Driver 2: [factor + logic]
- Dominant Theme: [label]
- Dominant Pillar Weight: [dominant pillar + ~60%]
- Retail Sentiment Override: [Yes / No]
- Market Regime: [label]
- Regime-Bias Alignment: [Aligned / Contradicts]
- Contradiction: [summary or "None"]
- Invalidation: [specific event or level]
- Optimal Trading Window: [session + time UTC + reason]
```

Using the research data below, write a comprehensive, highly detailed blog post explaining today's USDCAD fundamental bias so anyone reading it can fully understand the market conditions.
